constraints: Save Assign before trial; rollback after cost check.
content:
Skill: nearest_first
When: selecting which order to insert next.
Do: pick unserved order with minimum cal_dis() to any existing route. Try all Assigns, insert at position with smallest cost_delta after GenRoute().
Fallback: if no feasible position, create new Assign and force AddShip.
Safety: save Assign state before trial insert; RemoveShip + GenRoute to undo after cost check.
summary: Low-density d25 heuristic: select unserved order closest to any route, insert at min-cost position.
title: Nearest Insertion Skill Card

content:
Skill: farthest_first
When: orders are geographically dispersed (low/medium density).
Do: pick unserved order with MAXIMUM minimum-distance to any existing route. Insert at best-cost position across all Assigns.
Fallback: force-insert into existing/new Assign if no feasible position.
Safety: selection criterion (farthest) and insertion criterion (cheapest) are separate steps.
summary: Low-density dispersed heuristic: select order farthest from any route first, then insert at min-cost position.
title: Farthest Insertion Skill Card

content:
Skill: solomon_select
When: medium-to-high density, need global selection criterion.
Do: for each unserved order k: score = alpha1cal_dis(depot,k) - alpha2cost_delta_of_best_insertion. Select order with max score. Insert at its best position.
Fallback: if no feasible insertion, greedy force-insert any order.
Safety: default alpha1=1, alpha2=0. GenRoute after every trial add/remove.
summary: For d50/d75 medium-to-high density, use a global weighted-score selection criterion with cost-delta insertion.
title: Solomon I1 Skill Card

constraints: If one feasible position, insert now.
content:
Skill: regret2_select
When: multiple orders compete for limited route capacity (d50+ density).
Do: for each unserved order, compute best and second-best insertion cost_delta across all Assigns. regret = cost_2nd - cost_1st. Select order with max regret. Insert at best position.
Fallback: if only one feasible position exists, insert immediately.
Safety: GenRoute after every trial AddShip/RemoveShip pair for accurate cost_delta.
summary: For d50+ medium density and limited route capacity, use lookahead regret2 insertion before route slots vanish.
title: Regret-2 Insertion Skill Card

content:
Skill: savings_merge
When: deciding whether to add order to existing Assign or start new one.
Do: compute s(i,j)=cal_dis(depot,i)+cal_dis(depot,j)-cal_dis(i,j). Sort pairs by s descending. Merge highest-savings pairs into same Assign. Remaining unserved: greedy insert.
Fallback: orders with no positive-savings partner get their own Assign.
Safety: track served set; never insert same order twice.
summary: Route-consolidation heuristic: rank pair-savings, merge strong pairs into shared Assigns, then insert leftovers.
title: Clarke-Wright Savings Skill Card

constraints: Return one score per feasible bin.
Prefer low used-bin count over cosmetic residual balance.
content:
Skill: obp_first_fit
When: need a simple online packing baseline.
Do: give earlier feasible bins higher scores so the first feasible bin is selected.
Fallback: if remaining is empty, evaluator opens a new bin.
Safety: return len(remaining) finite scores only.
summary: Online bin packing baseline: prefer the earliest feasible bin to reduce new-bin creation.
title: OBP First Fit Skill Card

constraints: Return one score per feasible bin.
content:
Skill: obp_best_fit
When: objective is to minimize used bins in online bin packing.
Do: score each feasible bin by how full it becomes after placing item; prefer smallest residual capacity rem-item.
Fallback: if no bin is feasible, evaluator opens a new bin.
Safety: never score infeasible bins; remaining is already feasible.
summary: Best-fit scoring: prefer feasible bin with minimum residual capacity after placing the item.
title: OBP Best Fit Skill Card

constraints: Useful when small gaps should stay available.
content:
Skill: obp_worst_fit
When: many medium items arrive and over-tight packing may hurt later placements.
Do: prefer feasible bin with largest residual capacity after placing item; keep bins balanced instead of full.
Fallback: switch toward best-fit for large items above capacity/2.
Safety: output exactly len(remaining) scores.
summary: Worst-fit scoring: prefer larger residual capacity to keep bins balanced for later items.
title: OBP Worst Fit Skill Card

constraints: Keep score formula simple and bounded.
content:
Skill: obp_harmonic
When: item sizes vary across small, medium, and large classes.
Do: bucket item by size/capacity ratio; for large items use best-fit, for small items preserve medium gaps, for medium items penalize residual below item/2.
Fallback: default to best-fit score.
Safety: no randomness or lookahead.
summary: Harmonic-style scoring: adapt bin preference by item size class relative to capacity.
title: OBP Harmonic Skill Card

content:
Skill: obp_residual_poly
When: best-fit leaves many unusable tiny gaps.
Do: compute residual r=rem-item; reward tight fit but add penalty for residual ranges that are likely unusable, e.g. r near small isolated gaps.
Fallback: if formula ties, use best-fit.
Safety: scores must be finite for every feasible bin.
summary: FunSearch-style residual penalty: combine tight-fit reward with polynomial penalty on unusable gaps.
title: OBP FunSearch Residual Polynomial Skill Card

constraints: Use math functions only if stable.
Return finite scores for every feasible bin.
content:
Skill: obp_util_sqrt_exp
When: need smooth scoring beyond pure best-fit.
Do: combine utilization gain, sqrt-normalized residual, and exp decay penalty for awkward gaps. Prefer high utilization without creating many tiny unusable spaces.
Fallback: clamp to best-fit style score if expression is unstable.
Safety: avoid NaN/Inf.
summary: EoH-style smooth score: mix utilization, sqrt residual terms, and exp decay gap penalty.
title: OBP EoH Utilization Sqrt Exp Skill Card
